title: Chassalia subherbacea

This family includes a large number of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Chassalia subherbacea belongs to the order Gentianales. intro: Chassalia subherbacea, also known as the Small-Leaf Chassalia or Chassalia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
